short: NVIDIA's prebuilt, containerized microservices for deploying AI models anywhere.
definition: NVIDIA NIM is a set of prebuilt, optimized inference microservices that package a model together with an inference engine, standard APIs, and runtime dependencies into an enterprise-grade container. The goal is to make deploying generative AI as simple as a managed API while keeping the model self-hosted on your own NVIDIA infrastructure (cloud, data center, workstation, or RTX PC). Under the hood NIM is built on engines like Triton Inference Server, TensorRT, TensorRT-LLM, and vLLM/SGLang, and it exposes OpenAI-compatible endpoints.
